items.title: SpaceX vs. Tesla: Here's Which Elon Musk Stock I'd Buy Right Now
items.summary: The article compares SpaceX and Tesla as investment options, both expensive stocks betting on future profitability. SpaceX, newly public, is unprofitable with $8.7 billion in losses and negative free cash flow of ~$20 billion annually, though it has $70 billion in net cash. Tesla is more stable with $98 billion in annual revenue, positive operating margins of 4.9%, and $7 billion in positive free cash flow. The author recommends Tesla as the less risky choice between two very risky stocks, despite both betting on uncertain future prospects (AI for SpaceX, robotics for Tesla).

items.title: Lycia Therapeutics Raises $75 Million in Oversubscribed Financing and Strengthens Leadership Team with Appointment of Chief Financial Officer and General Counsel
items.summary: Lycia Therapeutics completed an oversubscribed $75 million Series D financing round co-led by Janus Henderson Investors and Balyasny Asset Management. The company appointed Amy Bachrodt as CFO and promoted Karen Flick to General Counsel. Proceeds will support clinical development of LCA-0061 for food allergy and LCA-0321 for Graves' disease.

items.title: Moderna Strengthens Commercial Strategy As Pipeline Advances
items.summary: Moderna announced organizational changes with President Stephen Hoge taking expanded oversight of R&D, manufacturing, and commercial operations. The company reported Q1 adjusted loss of $1.18 per share (better than expected) and revenue of $389 million (exceeding estimates). Moderna reiterated 2026 revenue growth guidance of up to 10% and expects potential launches of three new products in 2027-2028, including flu-COVID combination and norovirus vaccines. The company also anticipates key clinical milestones for its oncology and rare disease candidates.
